_meta.purpose: Immutable, append-only ledger of pre-committed LOVS methodology calibration points for the BDBV 2026 outbreak. Each block is a pin moment: a set of corridors committed on a fixed date, with a fixed model uncertainty range and a fixed resolution date. refresh_pipeline.py reads the active (unresolved) blocks and carries their points forward UNCHANGED into every snapshot; it never re-derives them from the current run's corridor ranking. That carry-forward is the pre-commitment contract the calibration scoring depends on.
doctrine: Pin once. A block's points, model ranges, pin date, and resolution date are never edited after commit.
Carry forward. A daily refresh re-emits the active points verbatim. Re-deriving them from freshly ranked corridors would silently overwrite an earlier commitment and break pre-commitment.
Append only. New calibration points are added as a NEW block at an explicit pin moment, never by mutating an existing block.
Resolve by appending. On a resolution date, score the due points against public reports and append the outcome; the original pinned range is never altered.

Early-lock when monotonic-safe. A point may have its outcome appended before its block's resolves_at iff a new lab-confirmed case appears in the target zone strictly inside [pinned_at, resolves_at]: the YES outcome is monotonic-safe (cannot flip back) and the append is structurally identical to a resolution-date append. The early-lock authorization applies only to YES outcomes; resolved_no outcomes wait for resolves_at to remove ambiguity about late-arriving evidence.
_meta.id_derivation: hypothesis_id = 'calibration-point:bdbv-uga-cod-2026:30d:' + sha256('bdbv-uga-cod-2026|{source}|{target}|{horizon_days}d|{pinned_at}')[:10]. refresh_pipeline._calibration_point_id reproduces this and verifies every point's id against its corridor at load time.
_meta.consumed_by: refresh_pipeline.carry_forward_calibration(as_of) -> writes data/live-bdbv-2026-output.json mode_b_hypotheses and resolves_at. See PIPELINE.md, section (c) Calibration and resolution.
_meta.licensing: CC BY 4.0 (per LICENSES.md) covers this file's schema, structure, and authored annotations. The model ranges are original derived outputs of the LOVS pipeline.

blocks.rationale: First calibration block. The four corridors ranked highest by ascertainment-adjusted 30-day export risk in the 20 May 2026 PHEIC-era snapshot, pinned as the method's pre-committed uncertainty test. Carried forward unchanged on every later refresh; scored against public reports on 2026-06-19.

points.resolution_provenance: calibration_resolver.py report 2026-05-26; early-lock monotonic-safe per ledger _meta.doctrine[3]

blocks.rationale: Second calibration block, pinned before publication of the 21 May 2026 snapshot. This block augments the initial top-four block with a designed sample across the current watchlist's relative high/mid/low bands, cross-border and in-country corridors, and likely positive / likely negative controls. Because the aggregate-input model still clusters all 18 watchlist corridors in a narrow probability band, these are relative tiers within the 21 May watchlist, not absolute low/medium/high probabilities.

points.resolution_provenance: calibration_resolver.py report 2026-05-26; early-lock monotonic-safe per ledger _meta.doctrine[3]
